# 🔍 PR Review: #13048 — replace github.com/golang/snappy with klauspost/compress/snappy

**Author**: @thaJeztah  
**Files changed**: 4 (+3 / -4)

### 📋 Summary

This PR replaces the deprecated `github.com/golang/snappy` dependency with `klauspost/compress/snappy` as a drop-in replacement. The archived `golang/snappy` repository is no longer maintained, and `klauspost/compress` is already an indirect dependency of the project. Changes are minimal: two import path replacements in Go source files and `go.mod`/`go.sum` updates.

### ⚠️ Risks

- **Low risk**: `klauspost/compress/snappy` is documented as a drop-in replacement — the API is identical
- The `go.sum` update removes the old snappy entry — ensure `go mod tidy` was run and the lock file is consistent

### 💡 Improvement Suggestions

- Run `go build ./...` and `go test ./...` to confirm no runtime regressions (the PR description doesn't mention test results)
- Verify the `klauspost/compress` version `v1.18.4` is compatible with the rest of the dependency tree
- Consider adding a `//go:build` tag or a comment explaining why this specific version is pinned, since it was previously an indirect dependency

### 🎯 Confidence Score

**High** — This is a routine maintenance swap with identical API surface. The author (thaJeztah) is a well-known Docker/GitHub CLI contributor and the change is minimal and well-documented.

### 📝 Additional Notes

The PR correctly relates to both the tracking issue (#11468) and the precedent PR in docker/buildx (#3686), which gives confidence this is a well-understood migration. The lack of test output in the PR description is the only minor gap — worth asking @thaJeztah to confirm CI passed before merge.
